mechanism_narrative: Higher federal funds rates increase the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly depressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in high-rate environments are not significantly lower than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate signals sudden labor market deterioration, triggering immediate risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in greater than 55% of threshold-crossing events, or the signal loses statistical significance after controlling for market volatility.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer demand strength, supporting corporate revenue growth and driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in RSAFS and HOUST fail to produce positive 60-day returns in greater than 60% of cases, or interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production during a monetary tightening cycle reflects constrained output capacity and margin compression, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines during tightening cycles yield positive 20-day returns in greater than 50% of observations, or the regime-conditional effect vanishes in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Elevated policy rates combined with slowing nonfarm payroll growth create stagflationary pressure that forces equity deleveraging, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint condition yields positive 20-day returns in greater than 55% of triggered months, or the interaction effect is dominated by single-series predictive power.

mechanism_narrative: Stronger quarterly real GDP growth directly correlates with higher corporate profit expectations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 5 trading days following data release.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth percentiles show no statistically significant separation in 5-day forward returns, or the signal reverses direction during recessionary periods.

mechanism_narrative: Nonfarm payroll additions dropping below 100,000 signals a structural labor market slowdown that reduces consumer spending capacity, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns remain positive in greater than 65% of months where payroll growth falls below the threshold, or the signal fails to survive transaction cost adjustments.

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer prices alongside contracting industrial production indicates cost-push inflation that erodes real margins, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The combination of rising CPIAUCSL and falling INDPRO yields positive 20-day returns in greater than 50% of cases, or the joint signal is subsumed by broader market beta factors.
